children.heading: State authority
children.label: 1 State authority
children.text: Subject to section 300gg–23 of this title, each State may require that health insurance issuers that issue, sell, renew, or offer health insurance coverage in the State in the individual or group market meet the requirements of this part and part D (other than ) with respect to such issuers.1section 300gg–121 of this titleSee References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Failure to implement provisions
children.label: 2 Failure to implement provisions
children.text: In the case of a determination by the Secretary that a State has failed to substantially enforce a provision (or provisions) in this part or part D (other than ) with respect to health insurance issuers in the State, the Secretary shall enforce such provision (or provisions) under subsection (b) insofar as they relate to the issuance, sale, renewal, and offering of health insurance coverage in connection with group health plans or individual health insurance coverage in such State.section 300gg–121 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: as provided under subsection (a)(2); and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to individual health insurance coverage or group health plans that are non-Federal governmental plans.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to the succeeding provisions of this subsection, any non-Federal governmental plan that is a group health plan and any health insurance issuer that fails to meet a provision of this part or part D (other than ) applicable to such plan or issuer is subject to a civil money penalty under this subsection.section 300gg–121 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: a health insurance issuer, the issuer is liable for such penalty, or

children.label: I
children.text: sponsored by 2 or more employers, the plan is liable for such penalty, or

children.label: II
children.text: not so sponsored, the employer is liable for such penalty.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The maximum amount of penalty imposed under this paragraph is $100 for each day for each individual with respect to which such a failure occurs.

children.heading: Considerations in imposition
children.label: ii Considerations in imposition
children.text: In determining the amount of any penalty to be assessed under this paragraph, the Secretary shall take into account the previous record of compliance of the entity being assessed with the applicable provisions of this part and part D (other than ) and the gravity of the violation.section 300gg–121 of this title

children.heading: Penalty not to apply where failure not discovered exercising reasonable diligence
children.label: I Penalty not to apply where failure not discovered exercising reasonable diligence
children.text: No civil money penalty shall be imposed under this paragraph on any failure during any period for which it is established to the satisfaction of the Secretary that none of the entities against whom the penalty would be imposed knew, or exercising reasonable diligence would have known, that such failure existed.

children.heading: Penalty not to apply to failures corrected within 30 days
children.label: II Penalty not to apply to failures corrected within 30 days
children.text: No civil money penalty shall be imposed under this paragraph on any failure if such failure was due to reasonable cause and not to willful neglect, and such failure is corrected during the 30-day period beginning on the first day any of the entities against whom the penalty would be imposed knew, or exercising reasonable diligence would have known, that such failure existed.

children.heading: Opportunity for hearing
children.label: i Opportunity for hearing
children.text: The entity assessed shall be afforded an opportunity for hearing by the Secretary upon request made within 30 days after the date of the issuance of a notice of assessment. In such hearing the decision shall be made on the record pursuant to . If no hearing is requested, the assessment shall constitute a final and unappealable order.section 554 of title 5

children.heading: Hearing procedure
children.label: ii Hearing procedure
children.text: If a hearing is requested, the initial agency decision shall be made by an administrative law judge, and such decision shall become the final order unless the Secretary modifies or vacates the decision. Notice of intent to modify or vacate the decision of the administrative law judge shall be issued to the parties within 30 days after the date of the decision of the judge. A final order which takes effect under this paragraph shall be subject to review only as provided under subparagraph (E).

children.heading: Filing of action for review
children.label: i Filing of action for review
children.text: Any entity against whom an order imposing a civil money penalty has been entered after an agency hearing under this paragraph may obtain review by the United States district court for any district in which such entity is located or the United States District Court for the District of Columbia by filing a notice of appeal in such court within 30 days from the date of such order, and simultaneously sending a copy of such notice by registered mail to the Secretary.

children.heading: Certification of administrative record
children.label: ii Certification of administrative record
children.text: The Secretary shall promptly certify and file in such court the record upon which the penalty was imposed.

children.heading: Standard for review
children.label: iii Standard for review
children.text: The findings of the Secretary shall be set aside only if found to be unsupported by substantial evidence as provided by .section 706(2)(E) of title 5

children.heading: Appeal
children.label: iv Appeal
children.text: Any final decision, order, or judgment of the district court concerning such review shall be subject to appeal as provided in chapter 83 of title 28.

children.heading: Failure to pay assessment; maintenance of action
children.label: F Failure to pay assessment; maintenance of action

children.heading: Failure to pay assessment
children.label: i Failure to pay assessment
children.text: If any entity fails to pay an assessment after it has become a final and unappealable order, or after the court has entered final judgment in favor of the Secretary, the Secretary shall refer the matter to the Attorney General who shall recover the amount assessed by action in the appropriate United States district court.

children.heading: Nonreviewability
children.label: ii Nonreviewability
children.text: In such action the validity and appropriateness of the final order imposing the penalty shall not be subject to review.

children.heading: Payment of penalties
children.label: G Payment of penalties
children.text: Except as otherwise provided, penalties collected under this paragraph shall be paid to the Secretary (or other officer) imposing the penalty and shall be available without appropriation and until expended for the purpose of enforcing the provisions with respect to which the penalty was imposed.

children.heading: Enforcement authority relating to genetic discrimination
children.label: 3 Enforcement authority relating to genetic discrimination

children.heading: General rule
children.label: A General rule
children.text: In the cases described in paragraph (1), notwithstanding the provisions of paragraph (2)(C), the succeeding subparagraphs of this paragraph shall apply with respect to an action under this subsection by the Secretary with respect to any failure of a health insurance issuer in connection with a group health plan, to meet the requirements of subsection (a)(1)(F), (b)(3), (c), or (d) of section 2702 or section 2701 or 2702(b)(1) with respect to genetic information in connection with the plan.111

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The amount of the penalty imposed under this paragraph shall be $100 for each day in the noncompliance period with respect to each participant or beneficiary to whom such failure relates.

children.label: I
children.text: beginning on the date such failure first occurs; and

children.label: II
children.text: ending on the date the failure is corrected.

children.heading: Minimum penalties where failure discovered
children.label: C Minimum penalties where failure discovered
I
children.text: which are not corrected before the date on which the plan receives a notice from the Secretary of such violation; and

children.label: II
children.text: which occurred or continued during the period involved;

children.heading: Higher minimum penalty where violations are more than de minimis
children.label: ii Higher minimum penalty where violations are more than de minimis
children.text: To the extent violations for which any person is liable under this paragraph for any year are more than de minimis, clause (i) shall be applied by substituting “$15,000” for “$2,500” with respect to such person.

children.heading: Penalty not to apply where failure not discovered exercising reasonable diligence
children.label: i Penalty not to apply where failure not discovered exercising reasonable diligence
children.text: No penalty shall be imposed by subparagraph (A) on any failure during any period for which it is established to the satisfaction of the Secretary that the person otherwise liable for such penalty did not know, and exercising reasonable diligence would not have known, that such failure existed.

children.heading: Penalty not to apply to failures corrected within certain periods
children.label: ii Penalty not to apply to failures corrected within certain periods
I
children.text: such failure was due to reasonable cause and not to willful neglect; and

children.label: II
children.text: such failure is corrected during the 30-day period beginning on the first date the person otherwise liable for such penalty knew, or exercising reasonable diligence would have known, that such failure existed.

children.heading: Overall limitation for unintentional failures
children.label: iii Overall limitation for unintentional failures
I
children.text: 10 percent of the aggregate amount paid or incurred by the employer (or predecessor employer) during the preceding taxable year for group health plans; or

children.heading: Waiver by Secretary
children.label: E Waiver by Secretary
children.text: In the case of a failure which is due to reasonable cause and not to willful neglect, the Secretary may waive part or all of the penalty imposed by subparagraph (A) to the extent that the payment of such penalty would be excessive relative to the failure involved.
